page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose, ARM-based instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture (AWS Neoverse-V2) running at 2.8 GHz, with 384 GB of system memory, yielding 4 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gd.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with high read and write bandwidth for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and gzip compression benchmarks placing in the average tier.

While the instance excels at multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and static web serving, it exhibits mixed results in large language model inference. Text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models ranks in the top tier, but text generation for smaller models (135M) falls into the bottom tier. The combination of high-density ARM cores, 5.7 TB of local NVMe storage, and high memory bandwidth makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose, ARM-based instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Neoverse-V2 (ARMv9) architecture running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 384 GB of memory, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a 40 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution speeds remain in the average tier. While small-model LLM text generation is weak, the instance delivers top-tier performance for larger model text generation, software compilation, and static web serving. The high resource density of dedicated ARM cores paired with substantial local NVMe storage offers a qualitatively cost-effective profile for multi-threaded workloads, high-throughput web servers, and database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, 5.7 TB NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is an ARM-based instance with 96 dedicated cores, 384 GB RAM, and 5.7 TB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance combining ninety-six dedicated cores with five point seven terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
384 GB of system memory paired with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
